item.title: Single-domain generalized object detection by balancing domain diversity and invariance
item.dc:creator: Zhenwei He
Hongsu Ni
Ling Lu
Luxin Jiang
item.prism:publicationName: Pattern Recognition
item.description: Single-domain generalization for object detection (S-DGOD) seeks to transfer learned representations from a single source domain to unseen target domains. While recent approaches have primarily focused on achieving feature invariance, they ignore that domain diversity also presents significant challenges for the task. First, such invariance-driven strategies often lead to the loss of domain-specific information, resulting in incomplete feature representations. Second, cross-domain feature alignment forces the model to overlook domain-specific discrepancies, thereby increasing the complexity of the training process. To address these limitations, this paper proposes the Diversity Invariant Detection Model (DIDM), which achieves a harmonious integration of domain-specific diversity and domain invariance. Our key idea is to learn the invariant representations by keeping the inherent domain-specific features. Specifically, we introduce the Diversity Learning Module (DLM). This module limits the invariant semantics while explicitly enhancing domain-specific feature representation through a proposed feature diversity loss. Furthermore, to ensure cross-domain invariance without sacrificing diversity, we incorporate the Weighted Aligning Module (WAM) to enable feature alignment while maintaining the discriminative domain-specific information. Extensive experiments on multiple diverse datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed model, achieving superior performance compared to existing methods. Published: 2026-06-06T06:32:48+00:00 Venue: Pattern Recognition Score: 0.823 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Zhenwei He; Hongsu Ni; Ling Lu; Luxin Jiang

Journal: Pattern Recognition

DOI: 10.1016/j.patcog.2026.114184

Relevance: 0.823 (must_read)

Single-domain generalization for object detection (S-DGOD) seeks to transfer learned representations from a single source domain to unseen target domains. While recent approaches have primarily focused on achieving feature invariance, they ignore that domain diversity also presents significant challenges for the task. First, such invariance-driven strategies often lead to the loss of domain-specific information, resulting in incomplete feature representations. Second, cross-domain feature alignment forces the model to overlook domain-specific discrepancies, thereby increasing the complexity of the training process. To address these limitations, this paper proposes the Diversity Invariant Detection Model (DIDM), which achieves a harmonious integration of domain-specific diversity and domain invariance. Our key idea is to learn the invariant representations by keeping the inherent domain-specific features. Specifically, we introduce the Diversity Learning Module (DLM). This module limits the invariant semantics while explicitly enhancing domain-specific feature representation through a proposed feature diversity loss. Furthermore, to ensure cross-domain invariance without sacrificing diversity, we incorporate the Weighted Aligning Module (WAM) to enable feature alignment while maintaining the discriminative domain-specific information. Extensive experiments on multiple diverse datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed model, achieving superior performance compared to existing methods.

item.title: Towards SAR-to-optical image translation via perception correlative learning and global-local feature collaboration
item.dc:creator: Yu Chen
Weida Zhan
Yichun Jiang
Depeng Zhu
Xiaoyu Xu
Jinxin Guo
Ziqiang Hao
item.prism:publicationName: Pattern Recognition
item.description: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) enables all-weather, day-and-night Earth observation, but it suffers from speckle noise and geometric distortions that hinder human visual interpretation. SAR-to-optical (S2O) image translation can enhance the interpretability of SAR images. However, existing methods often neglect scene complexity, leading to color distortion and detail loss, which degrade both visual quality and semantic consistency. To address these issues, this paper proposes the S2O image translation method based on perception correlative learning and global-local feature collaboration (PCGL-GAN). Specifically, the perception correlative learning is introduced to model perceptual similarity in the feature space, thereby improving visual consistency. The global-local cascade extractor is further designed to enable collaboration learning of global structures and fine-grained textures, enhancing the model’s ability to restore structure and preserve details in high-density scenes. In addition, the cross-scale collaboration compensation is proposed, which jointly exploits frequency-domain features and spatial attention to achieve deep multi-scale fusion and semantic refinement. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that the proposed PCGL-GAN achieves competitive overall performance on the SEN1-2, QXS-SAROPT, and SAR2Opt datasets. It consistently improves multiple evaluation metrics, while a certain degree of trade-off remains among different metrics, reflecting its balanced performance under a multi-objective optimization setting. The code and models are available on GitHub . Published: 2026-06-06T06:32:58+00:00 Venue: Pattern Recognition Score: 0.819 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yu Chen; Weida Zhan; Yichun Jiang; Depeng Zhu; Xiaoyu Xu; Jinxin Guo; Ziqiang Hao

Journal: Pattern Recognition

DOI: 10.1016/j.patcog.2026.114186

Relevance: 0.819 (must_read)

Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) enables all-weather, day-and-night Earth observation, but it suffers from speckle noise and geometric distortions that hinder human visual interpretation. SAR-to-optical (S2O) image translation can enhance the interpretability of SAR images. However, existing methods often neglect scene complexity, leading to color distortion and detail loss, which degrade both visual quality and semantic consistency. To address these issues, this paper proposes the S2O image translation method based on perception correlative learning and global-local feature collaboration (PCGL-GAN). Specifically, the perception correlative learning is introduced to model perceptual similarity in the feature space, thereby improving visual consistency. The global-local cascade extractor is further designed to enable collaboration learning of global structures and fine-grained textures, enhancing the model’s ability to restore structure and preserve details in high-density scenes. In addition, the cross-scale collaboration compensation is proposed, which jointly exploits frequency-domain features and spatial attention to achieve deep multi-scale fusion and semantic refinement. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that the proposed PCGL-GAN achieves competitive overall performance on the SEN1-2, QXS-SAROPT, and SAR2Opt datasets. It consistently improves multiple evaluation metrics, while a certain degree of trade-off remains among different metrics, reflecting its balanced performance under a multi-objective optimization setting. The code and models are available on GitHub .

item.title: Selecting and Distilling Cross-Label Models
item.dc:creator: Su Lu
Han-Jia Ye
De-Chuan Zhan
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
item.description: The practice of knowledge distillation, wherein pre-trained models – termed ”teachers” – serve to enhance the performance of subsequent ”student” models, has seen a surge in popularity. With a lot of pre-trained models available, there is a pressing need to investigate effective mechanisms for utilizing these resources optimally. This paper tackles two main challenges inherent in developing such mechanisms. The initial challenge lies in the effective selection of the most advantageous models from a vast pool, given that exhaustively testing each pre-trained model is impractical. The second challenge stems from the diversity of pre-trained models, as it is common for a pre-trained model to have a different label space than that of the current task. This necessitates the development of a universal model reuse approach capable of integrating any pre-trained model, regardless of its unknown label set. To address these issues, we introduce a dual-phase framework named “Selective Cross-Label Distillation.” The first phase, termed model assessment, evaluates the semantic similarity between a potential pre-trained model and the target model through optimal transport. By determining the transportation cost, we pinpoint candidate models with lower costs to serve as our source models. The second phase, knowledge reuse, concentrates on minimizing the transportation cost between the chosen source models and the target model. This dual-phase approach enables efficient model selection and bridges the semantic gap between pre-trained models and the target task, leading to enhanced performance. The effectiveness of our framework in model selection and knowledge reuse is validated by experimental results. Published: 2026-06-05T19:38:54+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence Score: 0.819 (must_read)

Authors: Su Lu; Han-Jia Ye; De-Chuan Zhan

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence

DOI: 10.1109/tpami.2026.3700851

Relevance: 0.819 (must_read)

The practice of knowledge distillation, wherein pre-trained models – termed ”teachers” – serve to enhance the performance of subsequent ”student” models, has seen a surge in popularity. With a lot of pre-trained models available, there is a pressing need to investigate effective mechanisms for utilizing these resources optimally. This paper tackles two main challenges inherent in developing such mechanisms. The initial challenge lies in the effective selection of the most advantageous models from a vast pool, given that exhaustively testing each pre-trained model is impractical. The second challenge stems from the diversity of pre-trained models, as it is common for a pre-trained model to have a different label space than that of the current task. This necessitates the development of a universal model reuse approach capable of integrating any pre-trained model, regardless of its unknown label set. To address these issues, we introduce a dual-phase framework named “Selective Cross-Label Distillation.” The first phase, termed model assessment, evaluates the semantic similarity between a potential pre-trained model and the target model through optimal transport. By determining the transportation cost, we pinpoint candidate models with lower costs to serve as our source models. The second phase, knowledge reuse, concentrates on minimizing the transportation cost between the chosen source models and the target model. This dual-phase approach enables efficient model selection and bridges the semantic gap between pre-trained models and the target task, leading to enhanced performance. The effectiveness of our framework in model selection and knowledge reuse is validated by experimental results.

item.title: TSF-DETR: Two-Stage Frequency-Domain Enhancement DETR for Small Object Detection in Remote Sensing
item.dc:creator: Yingjie Hu
Wu Le
Ming Lv
Zhenhong Jia
Jiajia Wang
Gang Zhou
Sensen Song
Faquan Song
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
item.description: Small object detection in remote sensing imagery is inherently constrained by several persistent factors within the conventional spatial domain. On one hand, the small proportion of tiny targets in the entire image results in sparse spatial feature representations that are easily submerged by complex background noise. On the other hand, effectively capturing long-range dependencies in dense-object scenarios remains difficult, which limits the model's capacity for global context modeling. Collectively, these factors represent significant performance bottlenecks for existing spatial-based object detection algorithms. To remedy these shortcomings, TSF-DETR, a novel detection framework anchored by a Two-Stage Frequency-Domain Enhancement strategy, is proposed in this study. Specifically, a Remote Sensing Frequency Enhancement Module (RSEM) is integrated within the backbone as the first stage to implement a hierarchical differentiated mechanism for frequency-domain feature refinement. In the subsequent neck stage, a Multi-Scale Frequency Domain (MSFD) module is leveraged to execute multi-scale and multi-orientational Gabor filtering, establishing a robust “Global FFT Enhancement – Local Gabor Optimization” synergy to suppress background noise. Furthermore, to fortify the model's global perception, a tailored Linear Multi-Head Self-Attention (LMSA) mechanism is incorporated. Distinguishable from standard linear attention designs, this modified LMSA enhances the model's ability to capture long-range dependencies and aggregate global semantic context, thereby improving detection precision while maintaining high computational efficiency. Extensive evaluations on four benchmark datasets—VisDrone2019, DIOR, RSOD and USOD—demonstrate that TSF-DETR outperforms the RT-DETR baseline and several state-of-the-art methods, validating its efficacy in strengthening small object representations and enhancing detection accuracy across diverse remote sensing scenarios. Published: 2026-06-05T19:39:20+00:00 Venue: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing Score: 0.811 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yingjie Hu; Wu Le; Ming Lv; Zhenhong Jia; Jiajia Wang; Gang Zhou; Sensen Song; Faquan Song

Journal: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/jstars.2026.3700481

Relevance: 0.811 (must_read)

Small object detection in remote sensing imagery is inherently constrained by several persistent factors within the conventional spatial domain. On one hand, the small proportion of tiny targets in the entire image results in sparse spatial feature representations that are easily submerged by complex background noise. On the other hand, effectively capturing long-range dependencies in dense-object scenarios remains difficult, which limits the model's capacity for global context modeling. Collectively, these factors represent significant performance bottlenecks for existing spatial-based object detection algorithms. To remedy these shortcomings, TSF-DETR, a novel detection framework anchored by a Two-Stage Frequency-Domain Enhancement strategy, is proposed in this study. Specifically, a Remote Sensing Frequency Enhancement Module (RSEM) is integrated within the backbone as the first stage to implement a hierarchical differentiated mechanism for frequency-domain feature refinement. In the subsequent neck stage, a Multi-Scale Frequency Domain (MSFD) module is leveraged to execute multi-scale and multi-orientational Gabor filtering, establishing a robust “Global FFT Enhancement – Local Gabor Optimization” synergy to suppress background noise. Furthermore, to fortify the model's global perception, a tailored Linear Multi-Head Self-Attention (LMSA) mechanism is incorporated. Distinguishable from standard linear attention designs, this modified LMSA enhances the model's ability to capture long-range dependencies and aggregate global semantic context, thereby improving detection precision while maintaining high computational efficiency. Extensive evaluations on four benchmark datasets—VisDrone2019, DIOR, RSOD and USOD—demonstrate that TSF-DETR outperforms the RT-DETR baseline and several state-of-the-art methods, validating its efficacy in strengthening small object representations and enhancing detection accuracy across diverse remote sensing scenarios.

item.title: Cross-Scenario Deraining Adaptation with Unpaired Data: Superpixel Structural Priors and Multi-Stage Pseudo-Rain Synthesize
item.dc:creator: Kangbo Zhao
Miaoxin Guan
Xiang Chen
Yukai Shi
Jinshan Pan
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
item.description: Image deraining plays a pivotal role in low-level computer vision, serving as a prerequisite for robust outdoor surveillance and autonomous driving systems. While deep learning paradigms have achieved remarkable success in firmly aligned settings, they often suffer from severe performance degradation when generalized to unseen Out-of-Distribution (OOD) scenarios. This failure stems primarily from the significant domain discrepancy between synthetic training datasets and the complex physical dynamics of real-world rain. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a pioneering cross-scenario deraining adaptation framework. Diverging from conventional approaches, our method obviates the requirements for paired rainy observations in the target domain, leveraging exclusively rain-free background images. We design a Superpixel Generation (Sup-Gen) module to extract stable structural priors from the source domain using Simple Linear Iterative Clustering. Subsequently, a Resolution-adaptive Fusion strategy is introduced to align these source structures with target backgrounds through texture similarity, ensuring the synthesis of diverse and realistic pseudo-data. Finally, we implement a pseudo-label re-Synthesize mechanism that employs multi-stage noise generation to simulate realistic rain streaks. This framework functions as a versatile plug-and-play module capable of seamless integration into arbitrary deraining architectures. Extensive experiments on state-of-the-art models demonstrate that our approach yields remarkable PSNR gains of up to 32% to 59% in OOD domains while significantly accelerating training convergence. Published: 2026-06-05T19:41:16+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology Score: 0.808 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Kangbo Zhao; Miaoxin Guan; Xiang Chen; Yukai Shi; Jinshan Pan

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology

DOI: 10.1109/tcsvt.2026.3700727

Relevance: 0.808 (must_read)

Image deraining plays a pivotal role in low-level computer vision, serving as a prerequisite for robust outdoor surveillance and autonomous driving systems. While deep learning paradigms have achieved remarkable success in firmly aligned settings, they often suffer from severe performance degradation when generalized to unseen Out-of-Distribution (OOD) scenarios. This failure stems primarily from the significant domain discrepancy between synthetic training datasets and the complex physical dynamics of real-world rain. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a pioneering cross-scenario deraining adaptation framework. Diverging from conventional approaches, our method obviates the requirements for paired rainy observations in the target domain, leveraging exclusively rain-free background images. We design a Superpixel Generation (Sup-Gen) module to extract stable structural priors from the source domain using Simple Linear Iterative Clustering. Subsequently, a Resolution-adaptive Fusion strategy is introduced to align these source structures with target backgrounds through texture similarity, ensuring the synthesis of diverse and realistic pseudo-data. Finally, we implement a pseudo-label re-Synthesize mechanism that employs multi-stage noise generation to simulate realistic rain streaks. This framework functions as a versatile plug-and-play module capable of seamless integration into arbitrary deraining architectures. Extensive experiments on state-of-the-art models demonstrate that our approach yields remarkable PSNR gains of up to 32% to 59% in OOD domains while significantly accelerating training convergence.

item.title: Optical-Guided Neural Collapse for SAR Few-Shot Class Incremental Learning
item.dc:creator: Fan Zhang
Sijin Zheng
Fei Ma
Qiang Yin
Yongsheng Zhou
Fei Gao
Xian Sun
item.prism:publicationName: arXiv
item.description: Few-shot class-incremental learning (FSCIL) in synthetic aperture radar imagery presents unique challenges due to severe data scarcity and SAR-specific variability. In particular, strong azimuth sensitivity in SAR induces large intra-class variation and inter-class confusion, and FSCIL sequential updates further lead to catastrophic forgetting of previously learned classes. Inspired by neural collapse, we propose an optical-guided SAR FSCIL framework, which derives orthogonal feature subspaces from a data-rich optical ATR dataset and uses them as geometric priors to guide SAR feature learning. SAR features are projected onto these orthogonal subspaces via principal angle constraints, effectively transferring discriminative structure from the optical to the SAR domain. Specifically, our projection loss and the classifier loss optimized with a frozen simplex-ETF geometry jointly induce neural collapse by concentrating features around class means while maintaining large inter-class angles. We evaluate the approach on a benchmark comprising an optical ATR dataset and a SAR ATR dataset with 24 target classes, organized into a base training session and seven incremental sessions. Compared with recent FSCIL methods including NCFSCIL and so on, our method achieves the highest final accuracy and a favorable trade-off between final performance and performance degradation. Moreover, neural collapse metrics show improved intra-class compactness and inter-class separability, indicating that the learned features more closely approximate the ideal simplex-ETF geometry. Published: 2026-06-03T07:09:20+00:00 Venue: arXiv Score: 0.807 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Fan Zhang; Sijin Zheng; Fei Ma; Qiang Yin; Yongsheng Zhou; Fei Gao; Xian Sun

Journal: arXiv

Relevance: 0.807 (must_read)

Few-shot class-incremental learning (FSCIL) in synthetic aperture radar imagery presents unique challenges due to severe data scarcity and SAR-specific variability. In particular, strong azimuth sensitivity in SAR induces large intra-class variation and inter-class confusion, and FSCIL sequential updates further lead to catastrophic forgetting of previously learned classes. Inspired by neural collapse, we propose an optical-guided SAR FSCIL framework, which derives orthogonal feature subspaces from a data-rich optical ATR dataset and uses them as geometric priors to guide SAR feature learning. SAR features are projected onto these orthogonal subspaces via principal angle constraints, effectively transferring discriminative structure from the optical to the SAR domain. Specifically, our projection loss and the classifier loss optimized with a frozen simplex-ETF geometry jointly induce neural collapse by concentrating features around class means while maintaining large inter-class angles. We evaluate the approach on a benchmark comprising an optical ATR dataset and a SAR ATR dataset with 24 target classes, organized into a base training session and seven incremental sessions. Compared with recent FSCIL methods including NCFSCIL and so on, our method achieves the highest final accuracy and a favorable trade-off between final performance and performance degradation. Moreover, neural collapse metrics show improved intra-class compactness and inter-class separability, indicating that the learned features more closely approximate the ideal simplex-ETF geometry.

item.title: Spatial-Frequency Enhancement and Scale-Decoupled Refinement Network for Salient Object Detection in ORSI
item.dc:creator: Jinyu Han
Mengyin Wang
Chenglong Zheng
Yaoyao Hou
Fuming Sun
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: Optical Remote Sensing Image Salient Object Detection (ORSI-SOD) focuses on identifying and localizing visually distinctive regions within complex scenes characterized by intricate structures and cluttered backgrounds. This task presents significant challenges due to: (1) substantial scale variations among objects, (2) inherent ambiguity in target appearance, (3) pervasive background noise interference, and (4) edge degradation caused by shadow effects. To address these challenges, we propose a novel Spatial-frequency Enhancement and Scale-decoupled Refinement Network, termed SESRNet. The SESRNet synergistically combines three key components: (1) dynamic state modeling, (2) frequency-aware feature representation, and (3) structure-enhanced attention mechanisms. Our approach specifically overcomes the limitations of conventional frequency-domain modeling while effectively capturing long-range contextual dependencies, thereby significantly improving salient object detection accuracy in optical remote sensing imagery. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that SESRNet outperforms the current state-of-the-art methods across three public datasets. Code and results will be available at https://github.com/hjy0518/SESRNet/. Published: 2026-06-05T19:38:56+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.806 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Jinyu Han; Mengyin Wang; Chenglong Zheng; Yaoyao Hou; Fuming Sun

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3700192

Relevance: 0.806 (must_read)

Optical Remote Sensing Image Salient Object Detection (ORSI-SOD) focuses on identifying and localizing visually distinctive regions within complex scenes characterized by intricate structures and cluttered backgrounds. This task presents significant challenges due to: (1) substantial scale variations among objects, (2) inherent ambiguity in target appearance, (3) pervasive background noise interference, and (4) edge degradation caused by shadow effects. To address these challenges, we propose a novel Spatial-frequency Enhancement and Scale-decoupled Refinement Network, termed SESRNet. The SESRNet synergistically combines three key components: (1) dynamic state modeling, (2) frequency-aware feature representation, and (3) structure-enhanced attention mechanisms. Our approach specifically overcomes the limitations of conventional frequency-domain modeling while effectively capturing long-range contextual dependencies, thereby significantly improving salient object detection accuracy in optical remote sensing imagery. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that SESRNet outperforms the current state-of-the-art methods across three public datasets. Code and results will be available at https://github.com/hjy0518/SESRNet/.

item.title: BiCPF: Bi-Conditional Parallel-Fusion Network for Composed Image Retrieval
item.dc:creator: Yong Luo
Jinrong Yang
Xingyu Yang
Daqing Liu
Minjing Dong
Mengya Han
Zhiwei Ye
Liang Huang
Dongjing Shan
item.prism:publicationName: Information Fusion
item.description: Composed Image Retrieval (CIR) aims to retrieve a target image given a reference image and a modification text. However, existing fusion-based methods often produce modality dominant queries that consistently lean toward a certain modality, thereby impairing the model’s ability to effectively handle diverse real-world scenarios. In contrast, humans performing composed image retrieval dynamically reinterpret visual content from the perspective of text while simultaneously grounding language understanding in visual context, forming a bidirectional cross-modal semantic construction process. Motivated by this, we propose a Bi-Conditional Parallel-Fusion Network ( BiCPF ) that models cross-modal semantic relations from two complementary perspectives: an image-conditioned branch interprets textual modifications within the visual context, while a text-conditioned branch reinterprets visual semantics guided by textual cues. Specifically, our BiCPF includes: 1) Bi-Conditional Parallel-Fusion Module that utilizes two parallel fusion layers to learn modality-grounded queries conditioned on the complementary modality; 2) Similarity Aggregation Module that dynamically balances the contributions of textual and visual similarities for adaptive retrieval to handle diverse retrieval scenarios. Extensive experiments conducted on three benchmarks demonstrate that our method outperforms existing state-of-the-art approaches, particularly in scenarios where treating one modality as dominance is insufficient for accurate retrieval. Published: 2026-06-05T16:25:07+00:00 Venue: Information Fusion Score: 0.804 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yong Luo; Jinrong Yang; Xingyu Yang; Daqing Liu; Minjing Dong; Mengya Han; Zhiwei Ye; Liang Huang; Dongjing Shan

Journal: Information Fusion

DOI: 10.1016/j.inffus.2026.104514

Relevance: 0.804 (must_read)

Composed Image Retrieval (CIR) aims to retrieve a target image given a reference image and a modification text. However, existing fusion-based methods often produce modality dominant queries that consistently lean toward a certain modality, thereby impairing the model’s ability to effectively handle diverse real-world scenarios. In contrast, humans performing composed image retrieval dynamically reinterpret visual content from the perspective of text while simultaneously grounding language understanding in visual context, forming a bidirectional cross-modal semantic construction process. Motivated by this, we propose a Bi-Conditional Parallel-Fusion Network ( BiCPF ) that models cross-modal semantic relations from two complementary perspectives: an image-conditioned branch interprets textual modifications within the visual context, while a text-conditioned branch reinterprets visual semantics guided by textual cues. Specifically, our BiCPF includes: 1) Bi-Conditional Parallel-Fusion Module that utilizes two parallel fusion layers to learn modality-grounded queries conditioned on the complementary modality; 2) Similarity Aggregation Module that dynamically balances the contributions of textual and visual similarities for adaptive retrieval to handle diverse retrieval scenarios. Extensive experiments conducted on three benchmarks demonstrate that our method outperforms existing state-of-the-art approaches, particularly in scenarios where treating one modality as dominance is insufficient for accurate retrieval.

item.title: DroneSplat+: Semantics-Enhanced 3D Gaussian Splatting for Robust 3D Reconstruction from In-the-Wild Drone Imagery
item.dc:creator: Jiadong Tang
Yu Gao
Dianyi Yang
Haolin Yu
Tianji Jiang
Mengyin Fu
Yufeng Yue
Yi Yang
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
item.description: Drones have become indispensable for reconstructing in-the-wild scenes due to their remarkable agility. Recent advances in radiance-field representations have achieved photorealistic rendering, opening new avenues for 3D reconstruction from drone imagery. However, limited view constraints hinder reliable geometric recovery, while dynamic distractors in the wild violate the multi-view consistency in radiance fields. In the wild, these two challenges are inextricably coupled. To address this coupled dilemma, we propose DroneSplat+, a novel framework that formulates semantics as a unifying hub to simultaneously resolve both issues. DroneSplat+ couples multiview stereo predictions with segmentation priors to impose semantic constraints that steer Gaussian optimization, enabling accurate geometry recovery under limited-view conditions. To suppress dynamics, DroneSplat+ introduces a dual-elimination strategy that combines adaptive local-global masking and labelbased localization to precisely identify and remove dynamic distractors from static scenes. We also provide a drone-captured 3D reconstruction benchmark dataset encompassing both dynamic and static scenes for comprehensive evaluation. Experimental results show that our method outperforms both 3DGS and NeRF baselines in reconstructing in-the-wild drone imagery. Project page: https://bityia.github.io/DroneSplat +/. Published: 2026-06-05T19:38:54+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence Score: 0.803 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Jiadong Tang; Yu Gao; Dianyi Yang; Haolin Yu; Tianji Jiang; Mengyin Fu; Yufeng Yue; Yi Yang

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence

DOI: 10.1109/tpami.2026.3700887

Relevance: 0.803 (must_read)

Drones have become indispensable for reconstructing in-the-wild scenes due to their remarkable agility. Recent advances in radiance-field representations have achieved photorealistic rendering, opening new avenues for 3D reconstruction from drone imagery. However, limited view constraints hinder reliable geometric recovery, while dynamic distractors in the wild violate the multi-view consistency in radiance fields. In the wild, these two challenges are inextricably coupled. To address this coupled dilemma, we propose DroneSplat+, a novel framework that formulates semantics as a unifying hub to simultaneously resolve both issues. DroneSplat+ couples multiview stereo predictions with segmentation priors to impose semantic constraints that steer Gaussian optimization, enabling accurate geometry recovery under limited-view conditions. To suppress dynamics, DroneSplat+ introduces a dual-elimination strategy that combines adaptive local-global masking and labelbased localization to precisely identify and remove dynamic distractors from static scenes. We also provide a drone-captured 3D reconstruction benchmark dataset encompassing both dynamic and static scenes for comprehensive evaluation. Experimental results show that our method outperforms both 3DGS and NeRF baselines in reconstructing in-the-wild drone imagery. Project page: https://bityia.github.io/DroneSplat +/.

item.title: Bridging Multi-Stages and Multi-Views for Low-quality Point Cloud Denoising
item.dc:creator: Wu Chen
Qiuping Jiang
Hehe Fan
Chao Huang
Chunmao Wang
Xiaojun Chang
Yi Yang
item.prism:publicationName: Information Fusion
item.description: As a fundamental 3D representation, raw point clouds often manifest as low-quality data due to sensor limitations, imperfect environmental factors, and unstable scanning processes, necessitating the denoising process to improve data reliability. Although multi-stage denoising models outperform single-stage ones, their simple stacking limits inter-stage information interaction. Moreover, reliance on a single point-based view leads to incomplete information representation. To address these issues, we propose a novel Point Cloud Denoising framework designed to Bridge Multi-Stages and Multi-Views (BSV-PCD) for more effective and robust denoising performance. First, we design a Cross-Stage Interaction (CSI) module that leverages fine-grained information from the previous stage to promote cross-stage interaction, ensuring well-structured guidance and robust representation throughout the denoising process. Second, to overcome the limitations of single-view learning and fully explore the representation power of multi-view geometric structures, a Point-Voxel Adaptive Convolution (PVAConv) is employed. Specifically, PVAConv adaptively models diverse inter-point relationships, while utilizing a Point-Voxel Integration (PVI) operation to facilitate reliable multi-view fusion. Extensive experiments on both synthetic datasets and real-world scanned data demonstrate that BSV-PCD effectively recovers high-fidelity representations from low-quality inputs, outperforming state-of-the-art methods. Moreover, experiments on downstream tasks highlight its significant advantages in enhancing high-level task performance. The code will be made publicly available. Published: 2026-06-06T04:03:38+00:00 Venue: Information Fusion Score: 0.799 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Wu Chen; Qiuping Jiang; Hehe Fan; Chao Huang; Chunmao Wang; Xiaojun Chang; Yi Yang

Journal: Information Fusion

DOI: 10.1016/j.inffus.2026.104531

Relevance: 0.799 (must_read)

As a fundamental 3D representation, raw point clouds often manifest as low-quality data due to sensor limitations, imperfect environmental factors, and unstable scanning processes, necessitating the denoising process to improve data reliability. Although multi-stage denoising models outperform single-stage ones, their simple stacking limits inter-stage information interaction. Moreover, reliance on a single point-based view leads to incomplete information representation. To address these issues, we propose a novel Point Cloud Denoising framework designed to Bridge Multi-Stages and Multi-Views (BSV-PCD) for more effective and robust denoising performance. First, we design a Cross-Stage Interaction (CSI) module that leverages fine-grained information from the previous stage to promote cross-stage interaction, ensuring well-structured guidance and robust representation throughout the denoising process. Second, to overcome the limitations of single-view learning and fully explore the representation power of multi-view geometric structures, a Point-Voxel Adaptive Convolution (PVAConv) is employed. Specifically, PVAConv adaptively models diverse inter-point relationships, while utilizing a Point-Voxel Integration (PVI) operation to facilitate reliable multi-view fusion. Extensive experiments on both synthetic datasets and real-world scanned data demonstrate that BSV-PCD effectively recovers high-fidelity representations from low-quality inputs, outperforming state-of-the-art methods. Moreover, experiments on downstream tasks highlight its significant advantages in enhancing high-level task performance. The code will be made publicly available.

item.title: MPQ-DMv2: Flexible Residual Mixed Precision Quantization for Low-Bit Diffusion Models With Temporal Distillation
item.dc:creator: Weilun Feng
Chuanguang Yang
Haotong Qin
Yuqi Li
Xiangqi Li
Zhulin An
Libo Huang
Boyu Diao
Fuzhen Zhuang
Michele Magno
Yongjun Xu
Yingli Tian
Tingwen Huang
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
item.description: Diffusion models have demonstrated remarkable performance on vision generation tasks. However, the high computational complexity hinders its wide application on edge devices. Quantization has emerged as a promising technique for inference acceleration and memory reduction. However, existing quantization methods do not generalize well under extremely low-bit (2-4 bit) quantization. Directly applying these methods will cause severe performance degradation. We identify that the existing quantization framework suffers from the outlier-unfriendly quantizer design, suboptimal initialization, and optimization strategy. We present MPQ-DMv2, an improved Mixed Precision Quantization framework for extremely low-bit Diffusion Models. For the quantization perspective, the imbalanced distribution caused by salient outliers is quantization-unfriendly for uniform quantizer. We propose Flexible Z-Order Residual Mixed Quantization that utilizes an efficient binary residual branch for flexible quant steps to handle salient error. For the optimization framework, we theoretically analyzed the convergence and optimality of the LoRA module and propose Object-Oriented Low-Rank Initialization to use prior quantization error for informative initialization. We then propose Memory-based Temporal Relation Distillation to construct an online time-aware pixel queue for long-term denoising temporal information distillation, which ensures the overall temporal consistency between quantized and full-precision model. Comprehensive experiments on various generation tasks show that our MPQ-DMv2 surpasses current SOTA methods by a great margin on different architectures, especially under extremely low-bit widths. Published: 2026-06-05T19:38:54+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence Score: 0.795 (must_read)

Authors: Weilun Feng; Chuanguang Yang; Haotong Qin; Yuqi Li; Xiangqi Li; Zhulin An; Libo Huang; Boyu Diao; Fuzhen Zhuang; Michele Magno; Yongjun Xu; Yingli Tian; Tingwen Huang

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence

DOI: 10.1109/tpami.2026.3700583

Relevance: 0.795 (must_read)

Diffusion models have demonstrated remarkable performance on vision generation tasks. However, the high computational complexity hinders its wide application on edge devices. Quantization has emerged as a promising technique for inference acceleration and memory reduction. However, existing quantization methods do not generalize well under extremely low-bit (2-4 bit) quantization. Directly applying these methods will cause severe performance degradation. We identify that the existing quantization framework suffers from the outlier-unfriendly quantizer design, suboptimal initialization, and optimization strategy. We present MPQ-DMv2, an improved Mixed Precision Quantization framework for extremely low-bit Diffusion Models. For the quantization perspective, the imbalanced distribution caused by salient outliers is quantization-unfriendly for uniform quantizer. We propose Flexible Z-Order Residual Mixed Quantization that utilizes an efficient binary residual branch for flexible quant steps to handle salient error. For the optimization framework, we theoretically analyzed the convergence and optimality of the LoRA module and propose Object-Oriented Low-Rank Initialization to use prior quantization error for informative initialization. We then propose Memory-based Temporal Relation Distillation to construct an online time-aware pixel queue for long-term denoising temporal information distillation, which ensures the overall temporal consistency between quantized and full-precision model. Comprehensive experiments on various generation tasks show that our MPQ-DMv2 surpasses current SOTA methods by a great margin on different architectures, especially under extremely low-bit widths.

item.title: MonoHPE: Monocular 3D Object Detection with Height Positional Encoding
item.dc:creator: Chenxi Zhu
Zhi Li
Zhenyu Dai
Long Zheng
Weidong Wang
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
item.description: Monocular 3D object detection in autonomous driving has achieved revolutionary breakthroughs, primarily attributed to integrating perspective projection and geometric priors. Despite the continuous surge in overall performance, we argue that the subtle yet critical challenge of insufficient geometric constraints in the height dimension has not been sufficiently dissected, resulting in a systematic deviation between the bounding box height and the actual projected height, thereby nonlinearly amplifying the error in depth estimation. To uncover the profound essence of this error amplification mechanism, this paper employs a differentiable analytical framework to dissect the ratio dynamics of depth perception in perspective projection, providing a principled explanation of the sensitivity of depth perception to height deviations. Inspired by these insights, we propose MonoHPE, a method based on a hierarchical task learning paradigm. This method explicitly strengthens the geometric associations in the height dimension through an ingenious position modeling mechanism, achieving a breakthrough leap in the precision of depth estimation. Furthermore, to enhance the model’s adaptability in complex environments, we innovatively introduce an adaptive activation-guided loss, which directs the network to focus on semantically dominant regions, effectively suppressing foreground interference and noise contamination. Experimental results demonstrate that our proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance on the KITTI dataset. Published: 2026-06-05T19:41:16+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology Score: 0.795 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Chenxi Zhu; Zhi Li; Zhenyu Dai; Long Zheng; Weidong Wang

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology

DOI: 10.1109/tcsvt.2026.3700175

Relevance: 0.795 (must_read)

Monocular 3D object detection in autonomous driving has achieved revolutionary breakthroughs, primarily attributed to integrating perspective projection and geometric priors. Despite the continuous surge in overall performance, we argue that the subtle yet critical challenge of insufficient geometric constraints in the height dimension has not been sufficiently dissected, resulting in a systematic deviation between the bounding box height and the actual projected height, thereby nonlinearly amplifying the error in depth estimation. To uncover the profound essence of this error amplification mechanism, this paper employs a differentiable analytical framework to dissect the ratio dynamics of depth perception in perspective projection, providing a principled explanation of the sensitivity of depth perception to height deviations. Inspired by these insights, we propose MonoHPE, a method based on a hierarchical task learning paradigm. This method explicitly strengthens the geometric associations in the height dimension through an ingenious position modeling mechanism, achieving a breakthrough leap in the precision of depth estimation. Furthermore, to enhance the model’s adaptability in complex environments, we innovatively introduce an adaptive activation-guided loss, which directs the network to focus on semantically dominant regions, effectively suppressing foreground interference and noise contamination. Experimental results demonstrate that our proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance on the KITTI dataset.

item.title: Accurate and Robust: Dynamic Image Refinement for Remote Sensing Visual Grounding
item.dc:creator: Xiaowen Zhang
Lingling Li
Long Sun
Licheng Jiao
Xu Liu
Fang Liu
Wenping Ma
Shuyuan Yang
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
item.description: Recent advancements in natural image visual grounding have shown promising results. However, existing approaches struggle with precise grounding in remote sensing (RS) images. This challenge arises from two key issues. First, the prevalence of small objects and dense spatial distributions in RS images makes accurate localization difficult. Second, the large domain gap and lack of contextual priors in RS data hinder language-vision alignment. In this paper, we propose RefineVG, a novel framework for fine-grained visual grounding in high-resolution RS images. Unlike methods that require training separate models for coarse localization and fine-grained refinement, RefineVG employs a unified model that iteratively refines predictions. Specifically, RefineVG first predicts a coarse bounding box using a multi-scale cross-modality encoder and a Transformer-based decoder, which progressively align and fuse visual and textual feature. Then, the predicted region is enlarged to capture additional contextual feature. Combined with only category information as text, the cropped region is then re-encoded by the same model to obtain a refined grounding result. Finally, a fusion block combines the coarse and refined predictions. To support this process, we propose a data augmentation strategy by randomly expanding ground truth bounding boxes in training dataset, simulating spatial uncertainty.Extensive experiment results show that RefineVG achieves outstanding performance on three benchmarks, outperforming the second-best method by 5.11% on the DIOR-RSVG dataset, 8.61% on the OPT-RSVG dataset, and 3.61% on the RSVG-HR dataset in P@0.9. Our code are available at https://github.com/XiaowZhang00/RefineVG. Published: 2026-06-05T19:39:59+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia Score: 0.792 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Xiaowen Zhang; Lingling Li; Long Sun; Licheng Jiao; Xu Liu; Fang Liu; Wenping Ma; Shuyuan Yang

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia

DOI: 10.1109/tmm.2026.3700769

Relevance: 0.792 (must_read)

Recent advancements in natural image visual grounding have shown promising results. However, existing approaches struggle with precise grounding in remote sensing (RS) images. This challenge arises from two key issues. First, the prevalence of small objects and dense spatial distributions in RS images makes accurate localization difficult. Second, the large domain gap and lack of contextual priors in RS data hinder language-vision alignment. In this paper, we propose RefineVG, a novel framework for fine-grained visual grounding in high-resolution RS images. Unlike methods that require training separate models for coarse localization and fine-grained refinement, RefineVG employs a unified model that iteratively refines predictions. Specifically, RefineVG first predicts a coarse bounding box using a multi-scale cross-modality encoder and a Transformer-based decoder, which progressively align and fuse visual and textual feature. Then, the predicted region is enlarged to capture additional contextual feature. Combined with only category information as text, the cropped region is then re-encoded by the same model to obtain a refined grounding result. Finally, a fusion block combines the coarse and refined predictions. To support this process, we propose a data augmentation strategy by randomly expanding ground truth bounding boxes in training dataset, simulating spatial uncertainty.Extensive experiment results show that RefineVG achieves outstanding performance on three benchmarks, outperforming the second-best method by 5.11% on the DIOR-RSVG dataset, 8.61% on the OPT-RSVG dataset, and 3.61% on the RSVG-HR dataset in P@0.9. Our code are available at https://github.com/XiaowZhang00/RefineVG.

item.title: UBA: A Unified Black-Box Adversarial Testing for Object Detection via Visualization-Based Contextual Reconstruction
item.dc:creator: Jiawei Liu
Weisi Luo
Chunrong Fang
Quanjun Zhang
Junyi Xie
Dezhi Li
Jiang Wang
Zhenyu Chen
item.prism:publicationName: International Journal of Computer Vision
item.description: Object detection (OD) is widely used in safety-critical applications, such as autonomous driving, where robustness on real-world applications is essential. However, OD models can be highly sensitive to subtle input changes, making robustness evaluation crucial through adversarial testing, particularly under practical transfer-based black-box conditions where attackers lack access to model parameters and gradients. Analyzing the adversarial robustness of domain-generalizable OD models faces three primary challenges: (1) defining a unified adversarial goal applicable to various OD architectures; (2) identifying sensitive regions vulnerable to adversarial attacks, and (3) generating natural adversarial examples. To bridge this gap, we propose UBA, a unified black-box adversarial testing method for OD models. First, we design the UBA loss, a unified adversarial goal integrating multiple OD metrics (confidence, IoU, classification) with adaptive weighting strategies to accommodate diverse OD models. Second, we propose the object detection heatmap unit (OBHU) that identifies sensitive regions using integrated-gradient attribution maps. Third, we implement the context-aware generative adversarial network (CaGAN) that performs adversarial contextual reconstruction on sensitive regions, producing natural adversarial examples. Experiments on eight OD models spanning one-stage, two-stage, and transformer-based architectures across three datasets (INRIA, VOC-900, KITTI) demonstrate UBA’s effectiveness. UBA outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving 57.22–61.74 mAP reduction and 58.49–69.29 recall reduction while maintaining superior visual quality (PSNR: 24.85–26.12, LPIPS: 0.151–0.186). Surrogate sensitivity analysis validates robust cross-architecture transferability (90–96%). Saliency method ablation confirms Integrated Gradients’ superiority (14.76–27.65 mAP advantage). Fine-tuning experiments show adversarial training with UBA examples improves clean-data detection by 13.7–28.9% across three test sets, confirming UBA effectively addresses adversarial robustness challenges in domain-generalizable OD models. Published: 2026-06-06T11:12:17+00:00 Venue: International Journal of Computer Vision Score: 0.791 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Jiawei Liu; Weisi Luo; Chunrong Fang; Quanjun Zhang; Junyi Xie; Dezhi Li; Jiang Wang; Zhenyu Chen

Journal: International Journal of Computer Vision

DOI: 10.1007/s11263-026-02890-4

Relevance: 0.791 (must_read)

Object detection (OD) is widely used in safety-critical applications, such as autonomous driving, where robustness on real-world applications is essential. However, OD models can be highly sensitive to subtle input changes, making robustness evaluation crucial through adversarial testing, particularly under practical transfer-based black-box conditions where attackers lack access to model parameters and gradients. Analyzing the adversarial robustness of domain-generalizable OD models faces three primary challenges: (1) defining a unified adversarial goal applicable to various OD architectures; (2) identifying sensitive regions vulnerable to adversarial attacks, and (3) generating natural adversarial examples. To bridge this gap, we propose UBA, a unified black-box adversarial testing method for OD models. First, we design the UBA loss, a unified adversarial goal integrating multiple OD metrics (confidence, IoU, classification) with adaptive weighting strategies to accommodate diverse OD models. Second, we propose the object detection heatmap unit (OBHU) that identifies sensitive regions using integrated-gradient attribution maps. Third, we implement the context-aware generative adversarial network (CaGAN) that performs adversarial contextual reconstruction on sensitive regions, producing natural adversarial examples. Experiments on eight OD models spanning one-stage, two-stage, and transformer-based architectures across three datasets (INRIA, VOC-900, KITTI) demonstrate UBA’s effectiveness. UBA outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving 57.22–61.74 mAP reduction and 58.49–69.29 recall reduction while maintaining superior visual quality (PSNR: 24.85–26.12, LPIPS: 0.151–0.186). Surrogate sensitivity analysis validates robust cross-architecture transferability (90–96%). Saliency method ablation confirms Integrated Gradients’ superiority (14.76–27.65 mAP advantage). Fine-tuning experiments show adversarial training with UBA examples improves clean-data detection by 13.7–28.9% across three test sets, confirming UBA effectively addresses adversarial robustness challenges in domain-generalizable OD models.

item.title: TRAN: Texture Response Adjustment Network for Lightweight Single Image Super-Resolution
item.dc:creator: Ping Zheng
Rongfeng Zhou
He Jiang
Wen Cui
Liangliang Chen
Qiqi Kou
Deqiang Cheng
Guangtao Zhai
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
item.description: Single Image Super-Resolution (SISR) aims to reconstruct a high-resolution (HR) image from a given low-resolution (LR) input. Reconstruction difficulty varies across textures of different complexity. However, existing methods apply a single, unified feature modeling and reconstruction pipeline for all textures. This often exacerbates the reconstruction imbalance across textures of varying complexity. To address this issue, this study proposes Texture Response Adjustment Network for SISR (TRAN). This method achieves more balanced fine-grained reconstruction and improves overall reconstruction quality by adaptively adjusting texture response. TRAN comprises two main components. The first mitigates local texture response bias within an image by decoupling textures of different complexity in the frequency domain for independent modeling and response adjustment. The second alleviates global texture response bias across different images by dynamically perceiving overall image complexity and adjusting the network structure accordingly. Experiments on multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed TRAN balances fine-grained reconstruction across textures of different complexity and improve overall reconstruction performance. Notably, on the ×4 SISR task of the Manga109 dataset, TRAN outperforms MambaIRv2-L by 0.31 dB in PSNR while reducing FLOPs by 38.1G. Published: 2026-06-05T19:41:16+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology Score: 0.791 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Ping Zheng; Rongfeng Zhou; He Jiang; Wen Cui; Liangliang Chen; Qiqi Kou; Deqiang Cheng; Guangtao Zhai

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology

DOI: 10.1109/tcsvt.2026.3700195

Relevance: 0.791 (must_read)

Single Image Super-Resolution (SISR) aims to reconstruct a high-resolution (HR) image from a given low-resolution (LR) input. Reconstruction difficulty varies across textures of different complexity. However, existing methods apply a single, unified feature modeling and reconstruction pipeline for all textures. This often exacerbates the reconstruction imbalance across textures of varying complexity. To address this issue, this study proposes Texture Response Adjustment Network for SISR (TRAN). This method achieves more balanced fine-grained reconstruction and improves overall reconstruction quality by adaptively adjusting texture response. TRAN comprises two main components. The first mitigates local texture response bias within an image by decoupling textures of different complexity in the frequency domain for independent modeling and response adjustment. The second alleviates global texture response bias across different images by dynamically perceiving overall image complexity and adjusting the network structure accordingly. Experiments on multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed TRAN balances fine-grained reconstruction across textures of different complexity and improve overall reconstruction performance. Notably, on the ×4 SISR task of the Manga109 dataset, TRAN outperforms MambaIRv2-L by 0.31 dB in PSNR while reducing FLOPs by 38.1G.

item.title: Semi-Supervised Semantic Segmentation of Remote Sensing Images with Foundation Model Distillation and Multi-Level Consistency
item.dc:creator: Pu Xiao
Xiaoyan Lu
Yao Sun
Yuting Dong
Ji Zhao
Yanfei Zhong
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: Semantic segmentation of remote sensing images plays a crucial role in urban planning, environmental monitoring, and ecosystem management. However, its development is often constrained by the high cost of obtaining pixel-level annotations. Existing semi-supervised learning approaches mitigate this problem by leveraging a small amount of labeled data together with abundant unlabeled data. However, in scenarios with limited annotations, their performance is often degraded by pseudo-label noise and the insufficient utilization of structural information in unlabeled data. To address these challenges, we propose FDMatch, a semi-supervised semantic segmentation framework for remote sensing images that integrates foundation model distillation with multi-level consistency learning. Specifically, FDMatch employs a pre-trained remote sensing foundation model as a knowledge source and transfers its rich spatial priors and high-level semantics to a lightweight segmentation network through an adaptive distillation mechanism, thereby enhancing the feature discrimination capability under limited supervision. Furthermore, a multi-level consistency regularization strategy is developed to overcome the limitation of conventional pixel-level consistency, which explores the potential structural information in unlabeled data by modeling inter-class dependency patterns and inter-pixel correlations, thereby providing informative and reliable supervisory signals for model optimization. Comprehensive evaluations on three benchmark remote sensing datasets demonstrate that FDMatch consistently outperforms existing semi-supervised methods across different label ratios, particularly exhibiting superior accuracy and robustness in scenarios with scarce labels. Published: 2026-06-05T19:38:56+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.791 (must_read)
