Authors: Pu Xiao; Xiaoyan Lu; Yao Sun; Yuting Dong; Ji Zhao; Yanfei Zhong

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3700394

Relevance: 0.791 (must_read)

Semantic segmentation of remote sensing images plays a crucial role in urban planning, environmental monitoring, and ecosystem management. However, its development is often constrained by the high cost of obtaining pixel-level annotations. Existing semi-supervised learning approaches mitigate this problem by leveraging a small amount of labeled data together with abundant unlabeled data. However, in scenarios with limited annotations, their performance is often degraded by pseudo-label noise and the insufficient utilization of structural information in unlabeled data. To address these challenges, we propose FDMatch, a semi-supervised semantic segmentation framework for remote sensing images that integrates foundation model distillation with multi-level consistency learning. Specifically, FDMatch employs a pre-trained remote sensing foundation model as a knowledge source and transfers its rich spatial priors and high-level semantics to a lightweight segmentation network through an adaptive distillation mechanism, thereby enhancing the feature discrimination capability under limited supervision. Furthermore, a multi-level consistency regularization strategy is developed to overcome the limitation of conventional pixel-level consistency, which explores the potential structural information in unlabeled data by modeling inter-class dependency patterns and inter-pixel correlations, thereby providing informative and reliable supervisory signals for model optimization. Comprehensive evaluations on three benchmark remote sensing datasets demonstrate that FDMatch consistently outperforms existing semi-supervised methods across different label ratios, particularly exhibiting superior accuracy and robustness in scenarios with scarce labels.

item.title: DifferSeg: Towards Diverse Multimodal Binary Segmentation via Differential Perception and Frequency Guidance
item.dc:creator: Qiangqiang Zhou
Jiawei Xu
Yong Chen
Dandan Zhu
Yugen Yi
Xiaoqi Zhao
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
item.description: In many binary segmentation tasks, most multimodal methods rely on fixed feature concatenation for cross-modal interaction and straightforward decoder designs dominated by low-frequency semantics. However, they ignore two key challenges: one is the lack of an adaptive mechanism to handle modality discrepancies and complementarity, and the other is the absence of an efficient decoding strategy to balance both high-and low-frequency representations. In this work, we propose a simple yet general multimodal binary segmentation framework, termed DifferSeg, to address both problems simultaneously. With the help of the differential perception fusion (DPF) module, DifferSeg employs learnable differential operators to adaptively align multimodal features and enhance their complementarity through residual fusion, effectively mitigating modality mismatch and fusion redundancy. In addition, we design a frequency-guided decoder (FGD) that builds cross-frequency interactions and multi-path upsampling to maintain consistency between detailed high-frequency structures and semantic low-frequency representations, ensuring fine-grained boundary recovery and noise suppression. Benefiting from these designs, DifferSeg can be easily generalized to diverse binary segmentation tasks, including both natural and medical modalities. Without bells and whistles, it consistently surpasses 67 state-of-the-art methods across 29 public datasets involving 18 downstream tasks, demonstrating superior generalization and segmentation accuracy. Code and pretrained models will be available at the link. Published: 2026-06-05T19:41:16+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology Score: 0.790 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Qiangqiang Zhou; Jiawei Xu; Yong Chen; Dandan Zhu; Yugen Yi; Xiaoqi Zhao

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology

DOI: 10.1109/tcsvt.2026.3700609

Relevance: 0.790 (must_read)

In many binary segmentation tasks, most multimodal methods rely on fixed feature concatenation for cross-modal interaction and straightforward decoder designs dominated by low-frequency semantics. However, they ignore two key challenges: one is the lack of an adaptive mechanism to handle modality discrepancies and complementarity, and the other is the absence of an efficient decoding strategy to balance both high-and low-frequency representations. In this work, we propose a simple yet general multimodal binary segmentation framework, termed DifferSeg, to address both problems simultaneously. With the help of the differential perception fusion (DPF) module, DifferSeg employs learnable differential operators to adaptively align multimodal features and enhance their complementarity through residual fusion, effectively mitigating modality mismatch and fusion redundancy. In addition, we design a frequency-guided decoder (FGD) that builds cross-frequency interactions and multi-path upsampling to maintain consistency between detailed high-frequency structures and semantic low-frequency representations, ensuring fine-grained boundary recovery and noise suppression. Benefiting from these designs, DifferSeg can be easily generalized to diverse binary segmentation tasks, including both natural and medical modalities. Without bells and whistles, it consistently surpasses 67 state-of-the-art methods across 29 public datasets involving 18 downstream tasks, demonstrating superior generalization and segmentation accuracy. Code and pretrained models will be available at the link.

item.title: Content-preserved augmentation and style-transferred normalization for single domain generalization
item.dc:creator: Weicheng Xie
Jingyu Hu
Chunlin Yan
Xilin He
Siyang Song
Linlin Shen
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
item.description: Although large progress has been achieved, current domain generalization methods still suffer from the domain gap between the source-domain and target-domain samples, e.g. the style cues of the source and target domains may differ much, which is particularly evident in the scenario with a single source domain. Data augmentation is one of prevailing approaches for producing diverse source-domain samples and narrowing this domain gap. However, the content cues of the augmentation samples by current data augmentation methods may be distorted too much, which makes network learning biased toward the classification-insensitive style cues. To address the content degradation and domain style gap, we propose the modules of Adversarial Content Consistent Normalization (ACCN) and style reconstruction-based Amplitude Normalization (SRAN) to reduce this domain gap while preserving content cues from a perspective of frequency domain. Our algorithm has two merits, (1) our ACCN can restore the content cues damaged in data augmentation, by regularizing the content representations of augmented samples to approach those of the original; (2) our SRAN can reduce the style gap between the source and target domains by modeling instance-aware style cues of source domain and transferring them onto the target-domain features. Our ACCN and SRAN are simple yet effective, and extensive results on five public benchmarks further demonstrate the effectiveness of our method in enhancing the generalization performance on unseen target domains. Our codes are available at https://github.com/MeteorDream/CASN Published: 2026-06-05T19:39:59+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia Score: 0.790 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Weicheng Xie; Jingyu Hu; Chunlin Yan; Xilin He; Siyang Song; Linlin Shen

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia

DOI: 10.1109/tmm.2026.3700788

Relevance: 0.790 (must_read)

Although large progress has been achieved, current domain generalization methods still suffer from the domain gap between the source-domain and target-domain samples, e.g. the style cues of the source and target domains may differ much, which is particularly evident in the scenario with a single source domain. Data augmentation is one of prevailing approaches for producing diverse source-domain samples and narrowing this domain gap. However, the content cues of the augmentation samples by current data augmentation methods may be distorted too much, which makes network learning biased toward the classification-insensitive style cues. To address the content degradation and domain style gap, we propose the modules of Adversarial Content Consistent Normalization (ACCN) and style reconstruction-based Amplitude Normalization (SRAN) to reduce this domain gap while preserving content cues from a perspective of frequency domain. Our algorithm has two merits, (1) our ACCN can restore the content cues damaged in data augmentation, by regularizing the content representations of augmented samples to approach those of the original; (2) our SRAN can reduce the style gap between the source and target domains by modeling instance-aware style cues of source domain and transferring them onto the target-domain features. Our ACCN and SRAN are simple yet effective, and extensive results on five public benchmarks further demonstrate the effectiveness of our method in enhancing the generalization performance on unseen target domains. Our codes are available at https://github.com/MeteorDream/CASN

item.title: A Unified Framework for Pseudo-Supervised Clustering via Weighted Sample Aggregation
item.dc:creator: Haonan Xin
Danyang Wu
Zhe Cao
Zihua Zhao
Yichen Wang
Rong Wang
Feiping Nie
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
item.description: Multi-view clustering methods based on graph learning have attracted considerable attention due to their superior clustering performance. However, such approaches generally lack effective supervisory signals, making it difficult to fully exploit the latent correlations among multi-view data. In addition, existing methods often treat all samples equally in local learning, which limits their ability to capture critical local structures. To address these issues, A Unified Framework for Pseudo-Supervised Clustering via Weighted Sample Aggregation (PSC-WSA) is proposed, which constructs a complete pseudo supervision-guided clustering framework encompassing pseudo supervision information generation, weighted sample aggregation, clustering, and label propagation. In this framework, the sample aggregation process is learnable under the constraints of pseudo-supervision, and synergistically interacts with the clustering process, serving as the key bridge for pseudo-supervision guided clustering. Moreover, two weighted aggregation strategies are designed to adaptively model local relationships between highly similar samples. This enables diverse sample-level locality learning across different views, thereby effectively enhancing the discriminability of the representative samples. To optimize the PSC-WSA model, an efficient alternating iterative optimization algorithm is developed. Extensive experiments on both multi view datasets and multimodal remote sensing datasets validate the feasibility, effectiveness and strong scalability. Published: 2026-06-05T19:38:54+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence Score: 0.790 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Haonan Xin; Danyang Wu; Zhe Cao; Zihua Zhao; Yichen Wang; Rong Wang; Feiping Nie

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence

DOI: 10.1109/tpami.2026.3700151

Relevance: 0.790 (must_read)

Multi-view clustering methods based on graph learning have attracted considerable attention due to their superior clustering performance. However, such approaches generally lack effective supervisory signals, making it difficult to fully exploit the latent correlations among multi-view data. In addition, existing methods often treat all samples equally in local learning, which limits their ability to capture critical local structures. To address these issues, A Unified Framework for Pseudo-Supervised Clustering via Weighted Sample Aggregation (PSC-WSA) is proposed, which constructs a complete pseudo supervision-guided clustering framework encompassing pseudo supervision information generation, weighted sample aggregation, clustering, and label propagation. In this framework, the sample aggregation process is learnable under the constraints of pseudo-supervision, and synergistically interacts with the clustering process, serving as the key bridge for pseudo-supervision guided clustering. Moreover, two weighted aggregation strategies are designed to adaptively model local relationships between highly similar samples. This enables diverse sample-level locality learning across different views, thereby effectively enhancing the discriminability of the representative samples. To optimize the PSC-WSA model, an efficient alternating iterative optimization algorithm is developed. Extensive experiments on both multi view datasets and multimodal remote sensing datasets validate the feasibility, effectiveness and strong scalability.

item.title: Breaking the Vicious Cycle: Coherent 3D Gaussian Splatting from Sparse and Motion-Blurred Views
item.dc:creator: Chaoran Feng
Zhankuo Xu
Yingtao Li
Jianbin Zhao
Jiashu Yang
Wangbo Yu
Li Yuan
Yonghong Tian
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
item.description: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) has emerged as a state-of-the-art method for novel view synthesis. However, its performance heavily relies on dense, high-quality input imagery, an assumption that is often violated in real-world applications, where data is typically sparse and motion-blurred. These two issues create a vicious cycle: sparse views ignore the multi-view constraints necessary to resolve motion blur, while motion blur erases high-frequency details crucial for aligning the limited views. Thus, reconstruction often fails catastrophically, with fragmented views and a low-frequency bias. To break this cycle, we introduce CoherentGS, a novel framework for high-fidelity 3D reconstruction from sparse and blurry images. Our key insight is to address these compound degradations using a dual-prior strategy. Specifically, we combine two pre-trained generative models: a specialized deblurring network for restoring sharp details and providing photometric guidance, and a diffusion model that offers geometric priors to fill in unobserved regions of the scene. This dual-prior strategy is supported by several key techniques, including a consistency-guided camera exploration module that adaptively guides the generative process, and a depth regularization loss that ensures geometric plausibility. We evaluate CoherentGS through both quantitative and qualitative experiments on synthetic and real-world scenes, using as few as 3, 6, and 9 input views. Our results demonstrate that CoherentGS significantly outperforms existing methods, setting a new state-of-the-art for this challenging task. The code and video demos are available at https://potatobigroom.github.io/CoherentGS/. Published: 2026-06-05T19:41:16+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology Score: 0.788 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Chaoran Feng; Zhankuo Xu; Yingtao Li; Jianbin Zhao; Jiashu Yang; Wangbo Yu; Li Yuan; Yonghong Tian

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology

DOI: 10.1109/tcsvt.2026.3700148

Relevance: 0.788 (must_read)

3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) has emerged as a state-of-the-art method for novel view synthesis. However, its performance heavily relies on dense, high-quality input imagery, an assumption that is often violated in real-world applications, where data is typically sparse and motion-blurred. These two issues create a vicious cycle: sparse views ignore the multi-view constraints necessary to resolve motion blur, while motion blur erases high-frequency details crucial for aligning the limited views. Thus, reconstruction often fails catastrophically, with fragmented views and a low-frequency bias. To break this cycle, we introduce CoherentGS, a novel framework for high-fidelity 3D reconstruction from sparse and blurry images. Our key insight is to address these compound degradations using a dual-prior strategy. Specifically, we combine two pre-trained generative models: a specialized deblurring network for restoring sharp details and providing photometric guidance, and a diffusion model that offers geometric priors to fill in unobserved regions of the scene. This dual-prior strategy is supported by several key techniques, including a consistency-guided camera exploration module that adaptively guides the generative process, and a depth regularization loss that ensures geometric plausibility. We evaluate CoherentGS through both quantitative and qualitative experiments on synthetic and real-world scenes, using as few as 3, 6, and 9 input views. Our results demonstrate that CoherentGS significantly outperforms existing methods, setting a new state-of-the-art for this challenging task. The code and video demos are available at https://potatobigroom.github.io/CoherentGS/.

item.title: HCDi-Fusion: Hybrid-Conditioned Diffusion Model for Cross-Resolution Multimodal Image Fusion
item.dc:creator: Duzhong Feng
Chenxi Qiu
Tao Yue
Xuemei Hu
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
item.description: In multimodal image fusion tasks, introducing diffusion models faces a critical challenge due to the lack of ground truth (GT) for step-wise supervision during the diffusion process. Existing methods typically unfold diffusion models in two ways: either supervising only the final fused result while neglecting intermediate diffusion steps, or adopting mask-based fusion strategies that rely on pre-generated masks to supervise intermediate predicted masks. However, the latter heavily depends on the quality of the pre-generated masks. Moreover, in real-world scenarios, the spatial resolutions of different modalities often exhibit significant discrepancies, which further complicates the generation of reliable pre-generated masks. To address these challenges, we propose HCDi-Fusion for cross-resolution multimodal image fusion. Specifically, the Hybrid-Conditioned Diffusion (HCD) model performs guided denoising by jointly leveraging self-conditioning and external conditioning, which considers inter-step relationships and different resolution discrepancies. Furthermore, the proposed Self-conditioned Step-Wise Fusion (SSWF) model enables progressive fusion in the latent feature space across diffusion steps. To accommodate the inherently unfolded nature of diffusion models in fusion tasks, we further design a Step-Wise Supervision Mechanism to supervise intermediate diffusion steps. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple fusion tasks, diverse resolution discrepancies, multiple datasets, and a wide range of evaluation metrics. Published: 2026-06-05T19:41:16+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology Score: 0.786 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Duzhong Feng; Chenxi Qiu; Tao Yue; Xuemei Hu

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology

DOI: 10.1109/tcsvt.2026.3700715

Relevance: 0.786 (must_read)

In multimodal image fusion tasks, introducing diffusion models faces a critical challenge due to the lack of ground truth (GT) for step-wise supervision during the diffusion process. Existing methods typically unfold diffusion models in two ways: either supervising only the final fused result while neglecting intermediate diffusion steps, or adopting mask-based fusion strategies that rely on pre-generated masks to supervise intermediate predicted masks. However, the latter heavily depends on the quality of the pre-generated masks. Moreover, in real-world scenarios, the spatial resolutions of different modalities often exhibit significant discrepancies, which further complicates the generation of reliable pre-generated masks. To address these challenges, we propose HCDi-Fusion for cross-resolution multimodal image fusion. Specifically, the Hybrid-Conditioned Diffusion (HCD) model performs guided denoising by jointly leveraging self-conditioning and external conditioning, which considers inter-step relationships and different resolution discrepancies. Furthermore, the proposed Self-conditioned Step-Wise Fusion (SSWF) model enables progressive fusion in the latent feature space across diffusion steps. To accommodate the inherently unfolded nature of diffusion models in fusion tasks, we further design a Step-Wise Supervision Mechanism to supervise intermediate diffusion steps. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple fusion tasks, diverse resolution discrepancies, multiple datasets, and a wide range of evaluation metrics.

item.title: Strong Saddle-Shaped Geometry of Depth Maps Can Boost Multi-View Stereo
item.dc:creator: Zekun Gu
Tiao Tan
Zongqing Lu
Jing-Hao Xue
Qingmin Liao
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
item.description: Multi-view stereo (MVS) aims to accurately estimate depth maps for high-quality 3D reconstruction. Recently, saddle-shaped depth maps have been proved to improve the quality of 3D reconstruction. However, existing methods often suffer from weak saddle-shaped geometry in the estimated depth maps and high redundancy in network design, leading to suboptimal reconstruction performance and low computational efficiency. In this paper, we propose SDMVSNet, a new model to show that stronger saddle-shaped depth map can achieve better MVS performance while maintaining high model efficiency. Specifically, we introduce a novel multi-granularity loss function to enforce strong and comprehensive saddle-shaped geometry of the depth map. Moreover, we propose a spatial folding regularization, which leverages the spectral bias of neural networks to produce smooth submaps for better reconstruction of saddle-shaped depth map. In addition, we propose a saddle-aware feature transformer to effectively capture long-range saddle-shaped geometric information via a lightweight attention mechanism. Extensive experiments on the DTU, BlendedMVS, Tanks and Temples and ETH3D datasets demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art MVS performance with high efficiency. Published: 2026-06-05T19:41:16+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology Score: 0.785 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Zekun Gu; Tiao Tan; Zongqing Lu; Jing-Hao Xue; Qingmin Liao

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology

DOI: 10.1109/tcsvt.2026.3700782

Relevance: 0.785 (must_read)

Multi-view stereo (MVS) aims to accurately estimate depth maps for high-quality 3D reconstruction. Recently, saddle-shaped depth maps have been proved to improve the quality of 3D reconstruction. However, existing methods often suffer from weak saddle-shaped geometry in the estimated depth maps and high redundancy in network design, leading to suboptimal reconstruction performance and low computational efficiency. In this paper, we propose SDMVSNet, a new model to show that stronger saddle-shaped depth map can achieve better MVS performance while maintaining high model efficiency. Specifically, we introduce a novel multi-granularity loss function to enforce strong and comprehensive saddle-shaped geometry of the depth map. Moreover, we propose a spatial folding regularization, which leverages the spectral bias of neural networks to produce smooth submaps for better reconstruction of saddle-shaped depth map. In addition, we propose a saddle-aware feature transformer to effectively capture long-range saddle-shaped geometric information via a lightweight attention mechanism. Extensive experiments on the DTU, BlendedMVS, Tanks and Temples and ETH3D datasets demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art MVS performance with high efficiency.

item.title: FSPA-Net: Frequency–structural prior guided dual-stream dynamic fusion for remote sensing scene classification
item.dc:creator: Yifeng Wan
Zhicheng Xiong
item.prism:publicationName: Knowledge-Based Systems
item.description: Remote sensing scene classification benefits from powerful spatial feature extractors, yet current methods often overlook the synergistic coupling of structural topology and frequency-domain characteristics, leading to the loss of fine-grained geometric details in complex multi-scale contexts. This paper presents FSPA-Net, a dual-stream architecture that synergistically exploits frequency and structural priors through dynamic cross-modal fusion. A lightweight frequency stream processes joint magnitude and phase spectra to yield discriminative spectral representations, which are progressively injected into the spatial backbone via multi-level cross-attention. We propose a Frequency-Aware Dynamic MLP Fusion (FDMF) module that conditionally generates scale-specific projection matrices and gating weights from compressed global frequency context, enabling content-adaptive feature recalibration. A novel Frequency–Structural Prior Attention (FSPA) mechanism is further introduced, explicitly incorporating Sobel-derived edge priors and high-frequency components to enhance spatially discriminative regions. Extensive experiments on the UC Merced Land Use, WHU-RS19, and NWPU-RESISC45 benchmarks demonstrate state-of-the-art performance, achieving 92.85% accuracy on NWPU-RESISC45 with only a 10% training ratio. Ablation studies and visualizations validate the contribution of each prior-guided component and the interpretability of the learned representations. Published: 2026-06-06T04:00:13+00:00 Venue: Knowledge-Based Systems Score: 0.784 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yifeng Wan; Zhicheng Xiong

Journal: Knowledge-Based Systems

DOI: 10.1016/j.knosys.2026.116343

Relevance: 0.784 (must_read)

Remote sensing scene classification benefits from powerful spatial feature extractors, yet current methods often overlook the synergistic coupling of structural topology and frequency-domain characteristics, leading to the loss of fine-grained geometric details in complex multi-scale contexts. This paper presents FSPA-Net, a dual-stream architecture that synergistically exploits frequency and structural priors through dynamic cross-modal fusion. A lightweight frequency stream processes joint magnitude and phase spectra to yield discriminative spectral representations, which are progressively injected into the spatial backbone via multi-level cross-attention. We propose a Frequency-Aware Dynamic MLP Fusion (FDMF) module that conditionally generates scale-specific projection matrices and gating weights from compressed global frequency context, enabling content-adaptive feature recalibration. A novel Frequency–Structural Prior Attention (FSPA) mechanism is further introduced, explicitly incorporating Sobel-derived edge priors and high-frequency components to enhance spatially discriminative regions. Extensive experiments on the UC Merced Land Use, WHU-RS19, and NWPU-RESISC45 benchmarks demonstrate state-of-the-art performance, achieving 92.85% accuracy on NWPU-RESISC45 with only a 10% training ratio. Ablation studies and visualizations validate the contribution of each prior-guided component and the interpretability of the learned representations.

item.title: FreqChange: Frequency-aware spatiotemporal learning with prior guidance for semantic change detection in remote sensing images
item.dc:creator: Haiyan Huang
Zhenfeng Shao
Duowang Zhu
Xiao Huang
Ruiqian Zhang
Qimin Cheng
Jindou Zhang
Yueming Duan
Deren Li
item.prism:publicationName: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
item.description: Semantic change detection plays a critical role in monitoring Earth’s surface dynamics by identifying the locations and semantic categories of changes from bi-temporal remote sensing images. Existing methods primarily employ a shared-weight image encoder coupled with a change extractor to detect changes in the spatial domain, while lacking sufficient temporal modeling and effective exploitation of semantic priors. As a result, they struggle to accurately localize changed regions and maintain intra-class semantic consistency. To address these limitations, we propose FreqChange, a frequency-aware spatiotemporal learning framework with semantic prior guidance. Specifically, a frequency change-aware module (FCAM) is integrated into the spatiotemporal learning framework to accurately perceive and localize changed regions by comprehensively analyzing both low- and high-frequency components between bi-temporal features. Additionally, to enhance the intra-class semantic consistency of bi-temporal images, we propose a semantic consistency enhancement module (SCEM), which leverages semantic priors derived from large-scale pre-trained vision foundation models, combined with semantic consistency upsampling, to effectively facilitate spatiotemporal learning. Through their collaborative integration, these modules collectively enable the precise detection of land cover changes from bi-temporal images while preserving intra-class semantic consistency. Extensive experiments on four well-recognized datasets ( i.e. , HRSCD, SECOND, HiUCD-mini, and Landsat-SCD) demonstrate that FreqChange consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both quantitative metrics and qualitative visualizations. By uniting frequency-domain analysis and semantic priors, FreqChange establishes a new paradigm for accurate and reliable semantic change detection in remote sensing. Published: 2026-06-05T17:20:29+00:00 Venue: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing Score: 0.784 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Haiyan Huang; Zhenfeng Shao; Duowang Zhu; Xiao Huang; Ruiqian Zhang; Qimin Cheng; Jindou Zhang; Yueming Duan; Deren Li

Journal: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1016/j.isprsjprs.2026.05.042

Relevance: 0.784 (must_read)

Semantic change detection plays a critical role in monitoring Earth’s surface dynamics by identifying the locations and semantic categories of changes from bi-temporal remote sensing images. Existing methods primarily employ a shared-weight image encoder coupled with a change extractor to detect changes in the spatial domain, while lacking sufficient temporal modeling and effective exploitation of semantic priors. As a result, they struggle to accurately localize changed regions and maintain intra-class semantic consistency. To address these limitations, we propose FreqChange, a frequency-aware spatiotemporal learning framework with semantic prior guidance. Specifically, a frequency change-aware module (FCAM) is integrated into the spatiotemporal learning framework to accurately perceive and localize changed regions by comprehensively analyzing both low- and high-frequency components between bi-temporal features. Additionally, to enhance the intra-class semantic consistency of bi-temporal images, we propose a semantic consistency enhancement module (SCEM), which leverages semantic priors derived from large-scale pre-trained vision foundation models, combined with semantic consistency upsampling, to effectively facilitate spatiotemporal learning. Through their collaborative integration, these modules collectively enable the precise detection of land cover changes from bi-temporal images while preserving intra-class semantic consistency. Extensive experiments on four well-recognized datasets ( i.e. , HRSCD, SECOND, HiUCD-mini, and Landsat-SCD) demonstrate that FreqChange consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both quantitative metrics and qualitative visualizations. By uniting frequency-domain analysis and semantic priors, FreqChange establishes a new paradigm for accurate and reliable semantic change detection in remote sensing.

item.title: Siamese Mamba with dynamic relational matcher for hyperspectral object tracking
item.dc:creator: Minghao Zhu
Chongchong Wang
Heng Wang
Yuebo Meng
Yaohai Lin
Junyi Li
Zongfang Ma
item.prism:publicationName: Pattern Recognition
item.description: Hyperspectral object tracking (HOT) faces significant challenges due to scarce data and the domain shift that arises when transferring RGB pre-trained models. To address these issues, we propose a novel two-stage framework SiamMDR, which introduces a task-specific pre-training strategy and a dynamic relational matching mechanism. First, we design a Siamese Masked Autoencoder (SMAE) that learns transferable spatio-temporal representations for tracking by reconstructing masked regions in video sequences using template guidance. Second, we develop a Z-shaped Multi-directional Scanning Mamba (ZMamba) block to efficiently capture long-range dependencies and a Dynamic Relational Matcher (DRM) that establishes fine-grained, background-suppressed correlations between template and search regions. Additionally, a Spectral Attention Aggregation Module (SAAM) is introduced to adapt features to the hyperspectral domain. Extensive experiments on HOT2020 and HOT2023 benchmarks demonstrate that SiamMDR achieves state-of-the-art performance, with a precision of 94% and success rate of 67.6%, outperforming existing methods by a significant margin while effectively mitigating the domain shift and template noise problems. Published: 2026-06-05T16:11:49+00:00 Venue: Pattern Recognition Score: 0.782 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Minghao Zhu; Chongchong Wang; Heng Wang; Yuebo Meng; Yaohai Lin; Junyi Li; Zongfang Ma

Journal: Pattern Recognition

DOI: 10.1016/j.patcog.2026.114083

Relevance: 0.782 (must_read)

Hyperspectral object tracking (HOT) faces significant challenges due to scarce data and the domain shift that arises when transferring RGB pre-trained models. To address these issues, we propose a novel two-stage framework SiamMDR, which introduces a task-specific pre-training strategy and a dynamic relational matching mechanism. First, we design a Siamese Masked Autoencoder (SMAE) that learns transferable spatio-temporal representations for tracking by reconstructing masked regions in video sequences using template guidance. Second, we develop a Z-shaped Multi-directional Scanning Mamba (ZMamba) block to efficiently capture long-range dependencies and a Dynamic Relational Matcher (DRM) that establishes fine-grained, background-suppressed correlations between template and search regions. Additionally, a Spectral Attention Aggregation Module (SAAM) is introduced to adapt features to the hyperspectral domain. Extensive experiments on HOT2020 and HOT2023 benchmarks demonstrate that SiamMDR achieves state-of-the-art performance, with a precision of 94% and success rate of 67.6%, outperforming existing methods by a significant margin while effectively mitigating the domain shift and template noise problems.

item.title: RePercENT: Scaling Disentangled Representation Learning Beyond Two Modalities
item.dc:creator: Vasiliki Rizou
Pascal Frossard
Dorina Thanou
item.prism:publicationName: arXiv
item.description: To leverage the full potential of multimodal data, we need representations that go beyond the state-of-the-art alignment and fusion approaches and exploit all cross-modal interactions without sacrificing modality-specific information. Learning disentangled representations is a principled way to identify these underlying shared and unique factors that are hidden in observational data. However, while multimodal disentanglement is a compelling paradigm, existing methods are largely confined to the two-modality regime due to its inherent scalability bottleneck. To address this, we propose RePercENT, a self-supervised framework designed to surpass these limitations and unlocks scalable pairwise disentanglement beyond two modalities. Through a multimodal `plug-and-play' architecture, our approach operates directly on pre-extracted embeddings, eliminating the need for extensive joint pre-training while making no assumptions regarding the underlying modalities or foundation model backbones. Moreover, we introduce a joint optimization objective for simultaneously deriving the shared and unique components, and provide formal theoretical guarantees that characterize the optimality of our solution. Across diverse modalities and tasks, RePercENT successfully recovers disentangled components while maintaining competitive performance and significantly reducing computational complexity. Published: 2026-06-03T17:10:39+00:00 Venue: arXiv Score: 0.781 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Vasiliki Rizou; Pascal Frossard; Dorina Thanou

Journal: arXiv

Relevance: 0.781 (must_read)

To leverage the full potential of multimodal data, we need representations that go beyond the state-of-the-art alignment and fusion approaches and exploit all cross-modal interactions without sacrificing modality-specific information. Learning disentangled representations is a principled way to identify these underlying shared and unique factors that are hidden in observational data. However, while multimodal disentanglement is a compelling paradigm, existing methods are largely confined to the two-modality regime due to its inherent scalability bottleneck. To address this, we propose RePercENT, a self-supervised framework designed to surpass these limitations and unlocks scalable pairwise disentanglement beyond two modalities. Through a multimodal `plug-and-play' architecture, our approach operates directly on pre-extracted embeddings, eliminating the need for extensive joint pre-training while making no assumptions regarding the underlying modalities or foundation model backbones. Moreover, we introduce a joint optimization objective for simultaneously deriving the shared and unique components, and provide formal theoretical guarantees that characterize the optimality of our solution. Across diverse modalities and tasks, RePercENT successfully recovers disentangled components while maintaining competitive performance and significantly reducing computational complexity.

item.title: Next-Generation Parallel Decoder for LPDR: Architectural Optimization and Class-Balanced GAN-Augmentation
item.dc:creator: Shawaiz Obaid
Nida Chandio
Neha Jamil
Muhammad Khuram Shahzad
item.prism:publicationName: arXiv
item.description: Real-Time License Plate Detection and Recognition (LPDR) forms the backbone of modern smart cities. Although the YOLOV5-PDLPR model substantially improved system efficiency through a parallel decoder approach, its performance is still affected by spatial character mismatches and data imbalance within the training set. This paper addresses these limitations by introducing Cross-Spatial Hybrid Attention (CSHA) and Class-Balanced Synthetic Augmentation (CBSA). An extensive study involving 75,000 synthetic samples is conducted and evaluated on four benchmarks: CCPD, CLPD, PKU, and an application-specific dataset. Experimental results demonstrate a substantial improvement in the recognition rate of minority provincial license plates from 78.2% to 91.5% while maintaining real-time processing performance of 152 FPS. The results indicate that spatially-aware parallel decoding combined with class-balanced augmentation provides an effective solution for high-speed license plate recognition systems. Published: 2026-06-04T07:16:06+00:00 Venue: arXiv Score: 0.780 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Shawaiz Obaid; Nida Chandio; Neha Jamil; Muhammad Khuram Shahzad

Journal: arXiv

Relevance: 0.780 (must_read)

Real-Time License Plate Detection and Recognition (LPDR) forms the backbone of modern smart cities. Although the YOLOV5-PDLPR model substantially improved system efficiency through a parallel decoder approach, its performance is still affected by spatial character mismatches and data imbalance within the training set. This paper addresses these limitations by introducing Cross-Spatial Hybrid Attention (CSHA) and Class-Balanced Synthetic Augmentation (CBSA). An extensive study involving 75,000 synthetic samples is conducted and evaluated on four benchmarks: CCPD, CLPD, PKU, and an application-specific dataset. Experimental results demonstrate a substantial improvement in the recognition rate of minority provincial license plates from 78.2% to 91.5% while maintaining real-time processing performance of 152 FPS. The results indicate that spatially-aware parallel decoding combined with class-balanced augmentation provides an effective solution for high-speed license plate recognition systems.

item.title: Multi-View Depth Estimation With Uncertainty Constraints for Virtual-Real Occlusion
item.dc:creator: Jia Liu
Bin Wang
Dapeng Chen
Hong Song
Zengwei Zhang
Nanxuan Huang
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
item.description: In the context of the rapid development of Augmented Reality (AR) technology, addressing the challenge of occlusion between virtual objects and real scenes becomes increasingly important. This paper focuses on depth-based occlusion handling methods for AR applications and proposes a multi-view depth estimation model. To improve the accuracy of object depth contour estimation, a Bayesian Convolutional Uncertainty Estimation module is incorporated into the model, and a new uncertainty loss function is introduced to constrain depth contours. Additionally, an Attention Spatial Convolution Fusion module is integrated into the feature encoder to enhance feature representation capability. Finally, an optional post-processing method, the Depth Edge Padding module, is provided. This module aims to optimize the performance of the depth map output by the model at object edges. The model demonstrates superior depth estimation performance on the ScanNet and 7Scenes datasets compared to existing multi-view depth estimation research, particularly exhibiting higher accuracy in object contour regions. Moreover, the model also exhibits more realistic effects in occlusion handling between virtual and real scenes. Published: 2026-06-05T19:39:59+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia Score: 0.780 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Jia Liu; Bin Wang; Dapeng Chen; Hong Song; Zengwei Zhang; Nanxuan Huang

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia

DOI: 10.1109/tmm.2026.3700821

Relevance: 0.780 (must_read)

In the context of the rapid development of Augmented Reality (AR) technology, addressing the challenge of occlusion between virtual objects and real scenes becomes increasingly important. This paper focuses on depth-based occlusion handling methods for AR applications and proposes a multi-view depth estimation model. To improve the accuracy of object depth contour estimation, a Bayesian Convolutional Uncertainty Estimation module is incorporated into the model, and a new uncertainty loss function is introduced to constrain depth contours. Additionally, an Attention Spatial Convolution Fusion module is integrated into the feature encoder to enhance feature representation capability. Finally, an optional post-processing method, the Depth Edge Padding module, is provided. This module aims to optimize the performance of the depth map output by the model at object edges. The model demonstrates superior depth estimation performance on the ScanNet and 7Scenes datasets compared to existing multi-view depth estimation research, particularly exhibiting higher accuracy in object contour regions. Moreover, the model also exhibits more realistic effects in occlusion handling between virtual and real scenes.

item.title: Unveiling the Unknown: Open Vocabulary Object Detection with Scene Graphs
item.dc:creator: Yi Chen
Yinghao Lu
Zhehao Li
Chenchen Yan
Jiafei Wu
Chong Wang
Jiangbo Qian
item.prism:publicationName: arXiv
item.description: Open-vocabulary object detection seeks to identify novel object categories that were not part of the training data. Many knowledge distillation-based approaches have shown promising performance by transferring knowledge from pre-trained vision-language models to object detection. However, these methods often overlook structured, image-specific relationships between objects, such as interactions and spatial arrangements. This oversight can significantly restrict the effectiveness of detecting novel categories. To address this issue, we propose a Scene-guided Relational Modeling detection framework. This framework utilizes scene graphs to capture structured semantic and spatial relationships between candidate regions and their contextual objects. It explicitly models interactions among neighboring regions and incorporates a Relation Attention Module to implicitly amplify the key relational cues extracted from the scene graph. Furthermore, we present a scene-based textual alignment branch that distills category knowledge from captions to guide relational alignment. This approach facilitates a seamless integration of visual relations with semantic information for enhanced detection performance. Comprehensive experiments show that our model achieves superior performance compared to other OVOD methods, improving the AP for novel categories on COCO and LVIS datasets. Published: 2026-06-04T09:23:08+00:00 Venue: arXiv Score: 0.780 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yi Chen; Yinghao Lu; Zhehao Li; Chenchen Yan; Jiafei Wu; Chong Wang; Jiangbo Qian

Journal: arXiv

Relevance: 0.780 (must_read)

Open-vocabulary object detection seeks to identify novel object categories that were not part of the training data. Many knowledge distillation-based approaches have shown promising performance by transferring knowledge from pre-trained vision-language models to object detection. However, these methods often overlook structured, image-specific relationships between objects, such as interactions and spatial arrangements. This oversight can significantly restrict the effectiveness of detecting novel categories. To address this issue, we propose a Scene-guided Relational Modeling detection framework. This framework utilizes scene graphs to capture structured semantic and spatial relationships between candidate regions and their contextual objects. It explicitly models interactions among neighboring regions and incorporates a Relation Attention Module to implicitly amplify the key relational cues extracted from the scene graph. Furthermore, we present a scene-based textual alignment branch that distills category knowledge from captions to guide relational alignment. This approach facilitates a seamless integration of visual relations with semantic information for enhanced detection performance. Comprehensive experiments show that our model achieves superior performance compared to other OVOD methods, improving the AP for novel categories on COCO and LVIS datasets.

item.title: ProMML for robust few-shot adaptation of vision-language models
item.dc:creator: Rongfeng Zhao
Li Zhou
Zonghan Shang
Yiwen Liu
Yugui Zhang
Shuang Li
Jin Ning
Xin Ning
item.prism:publicationName: Pattern Recognition
item.description: Large-scale pre-trained vision–language models (VLMs) enable strong zero-shot transfer. However, parameter-efficient few-shot adaptation often overfits limited training samples and generalizes poorly across domains. We propose ProMML, a unified adaptation framework built on a frozen CLIP backbone, which improves information flow through progressive representation fusion. ProMML integrates Progressive Layer Fusion (PLF), Cross-Modal Fusion (CMF), and a Dynamic Prompt Generator (DPG) over learnable layer-wise representation tokens. PLF aggregates these tokens by cross-layer attention, CMF performs lightweight bidirectional fusion over representation tokens, and DPG provides auxiliary dynamic soft-prompt regularization by combining image-conditioned prompts with a shared prompt pool. Experiments on 11 datasets show competitive performance, achieving an average harmonic mean of 81.63% for base-to-novel generalization and 70.33% average accuracy for cross-dataset transfer, while remaining competitive on ImageNet domain generalization variants. Ablations verify that the three components are complementary and that multi-level fusion is important for novel-class generalization. Published: 2026-06-05T16:12:15+00:00 Venue: Pattern Recognition Score: 0.780 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Rongfeng Zhao; Li Zhou; Zonghan Shang; Yiwen Liu; Yugui Zhang; Shuang Li; Jin Ning; Xin Ning

Journal: Pattern Recognition

DOI: 10.1016/j.patcog.2026.114160

Relevance: 0.780 (must_read)

Large-scale pre-trained vision–language models (VLMs) enable strong zero-shot transfer. However, parameter-efficient few-shot adaptation often overfits limited training samples and generalizes poorly across domains. We propose ProMML, a unified adaptation framework built on a frozen CLIP backbone, which improves information flow through progressive representation fusion. ProMML integrates Progressive Layer Fusion (PLF), Cross-Modal Fusion (CMF), and a Dynamic Prompt Generator (DPG) over learnable layer-wise representation tokens. PLF aggregates these tokens by cross-layer attention, CMF performs lightweight bidirectional fusion over representation tokens, and DPG provides auxiliary dynamic soft-prompt regularization by combining image-conditioned prompts with a shared prompt pool. Experiments on 11 datasets show competitive performance, achieving an average harmonic mean of 81.63% for base-to-novel generalization and 70.33% average accuracy for cross-dataset transfer, while remaining competitive on ImageNet domain generalization variants. Ablations verify that the three components are complementary and that multi-level fusion is important for novel-class generalization.
